payload.description: Dedicated bus lanes on 23rd Street. Westbound bus lanes from 1st Ave to 8th Ave. Eastbound bus lanes from 10th Ave to 2nd Ave. Bus lanes in effect 24/7 except westbound between 7th and 8th Ave.

payload.description: New turn restrictions on 23rd Street: LT – EB 8th Ave, LT – Lexington Ave, LT – Broadway, RT – 6 Ave. In addition to existing restrictions: LT – WB 9th Ave, LT – WB 7th Ave, LT – EB 6th Ave, LT – WB 5th Ave, LT – EB/WB Park Ave S.

payload.claim_text: 9 SBS routes carrying more than 250,000 daily riders. Reduced travel time from 10 – 25% faster bus speeds. 95% customer satisfaction. Safer streets leading to 20% reduction in all crashes.
payload.description: Overview of Select Bus Service benefits from the presentation

evidence_refs.source_quote: • 9 SBS routes carrying more than 250,000 daily riders • Reduced travel time from 10 – 25% faster bus speeds • 95% customer satisfaction • Better bus stop waiting environments • Safer streets leading to 20% reduction in all crashes

payload.claim_text: Community Board 4 suggestions/concerns and agency responses: Right turn bays to move turning vehicles out of bus lane – insufficient curb lane width, DOT exploring daylighting alternatives. Eastbound 10th Ave left turn signal – DOT studying addition. Additional mid-block pedestrian crossings – would result in slower buses, not included. Parking regulation suggestions – most consistent with DOT proposed changes. High number of buses laying over – NYCT to increase service frequency and improve on-time performance.
payload.description: Summary of CB4 questions, suggestions, and agency responses from the presentation

evidence_refs.source_quote: Right turn bays to move turning vehicles out of bus lane Insufficient curb lane width – DOT is exploring daylighting alternatives Eastbound 10th Ave left turn signal Additional mid-block pedestrian crossings Parking regulation suggestions High number of buses laying over

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

payload.claim_text: CB4 suggestions and agency responses on turn bays, curb lane width, left turn signals, pedestrian crossings, parking, and bus layovers
payload.description: Community Board 4 questions and agency responses table

evidence_refs.source_quote: Community Board Suggestion/Concern Agency Response Right turn bays to move turning vehicles out of bus lane Insufficient curb lane width – DOT is exploring daylighting alternatives Eastbound 10 th Ave left turn signal DOT is studying addition of signal as well as exploring alternative treatments to improve pedestrian safety Additional mid-block pedestrian crossings Addition would result in slower buses; not being included in this project Parking regulation suggestions Most were consistent with DOT proposed changes; 10 th – 9 th Ave is primarily residential High number of buses laying over NYCT to increase service frequency and improve on-time performance and reliability especially at start of route

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

payload.claim_text: CB4 suggestions and agency responses: Right turn bays, curb lane width (DOT exploring daylighting), Eastbound 10th Ave left turn signal (DOT studying), Additional mid-block pedestrian crossings (not included, would slow buses), Parking regulation suggestions (mostly consistent), High number of buses laying over (NYCT to increase service frequency).
payload.description: Community Board 4 questions and agency responses table
